internal_anchors.details: test.js Section 3 passed (3614/1 — single failure is staleness check, not anchors). No broken href anchors or showTab() calls.

tab_structure.details: test.js Sections 1/2/4/5/6/7/8/9 all pass. Tab structure valid.

external_links.details: Checked key data-source links: NOAA, NCEI, NASA, ESA/Gaia, INTERMAGNET, GRACE-FO, dome site, our site, GitHub. All reachable. noaa.gov returned 403 to automated user-agent but 200 with browser UA — bot-blocking false positive, not a broken link. Confirmed on second attempt.

mismatches: HTML built at 17:26 May 16; wins.json+sections.json updated at 21:20 May 16 by decider run. HTML is stale by ~3h54m.
HTML has 69 win anchors (id='winNNN'); wins.json has 71 entries (69 base WINs + 57b + 58b). The two new items from decider run are missing from published HTML.

HTML prose says 'six registry-level concessions' (SEC-4.6); fresh build from current sections.json says 'five' — the ISS-2041 'six to five' patch was applied to sections.json but HTML was never rebuilt.
HTML missing WIN-052 defense preemption paragraph (EXP-407 integration applied to sections.json but not rebuilt to HTML).
data_prose_consistency.details: Root cause: decider run 2026-05-16T21:20 applied 7 patches and updated wins.json/sections.json, but PUSH failed (403). Rescue copies went to FUSE; workspace-sync pushed data files to git at 01:06 May 17. HTML was never rebuilt after the patch run. Diff between fresh build and published HTML: 27 lines across 5 locations (date stamp trivial; 4 substantive content differences).

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WINs in wins.json have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Verdict distribution: Refuted by Data=11, Self-Contradicted=20, Misleading=16, Std Model Explains=18, Not Demonstrated=3, Unfalsifiable=3. new_in_v51 count=32. uncounted-failures.json has 18 entries matching HTML '18 acknowledged failures'.

build_reproducibility.details: Fresh build from git HEAD (same wins.json/sections.json as workspace) produces output differing from published docs/index.html in 5 locations: (1) date stamp May 17 vs May 16 [trivial]; (2) lines 1898-1899 — WIN-052 defense preemption paragraph present in fresh build, absent in published; (3) 'five' vs 'six' registry-level concessions in SEC-4.6 (4 prose references); (4) '69 confirmed claims' vs '69+ wins total' in SEC-4.6 sub-table lead-in. Published file is 1591634 bytes; fresh build is 1581209 bytes (10.4KB smaller — published has more stale content).

issues_found.description: Published docs/index.html is stale. Decider run 2026-05-16T21:20 applied 7 patches (sections.json + wins.json updated at 21:20), but the push failed (403). workspace-sync rescued data files to git at 01:06 May 17 but HTML was never rebuilt. Live site missing: WIN-052 defense preemption paragraph (EXP-407), 'six'→'five' sweep in SEC-4.6 (ISS-2041), sub-table lead-in fix (ISS-2042), and the 2 WINs' HTML renderings if any were added. HTML last built: 2026-05-16T17:26. Data last updated: 2026-05-16T21:20.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run 'node build.js publish' from a clean clone. This will rebuild HTML from current wins.json/sections.json and push to GitHub Pages.

issues_found.description: expansion-tracker.json next_id=395 but max live ID=407 (EXP-407 in live). COLLISION IMMINENT: next expansion allocation will produce EXP-395, colliding with existing EXP-395.json, EXP-396.json, EXP-397.json, EXP-398.json on disk. Root cause: a writer likely used items.length or a live-only max instead of the next_id field when allocating EXP-391 through EXP-407.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Set next_id to 411 (max_id 407 + 1 + 3 safety buffer) immediately. The decider should patch next_id before any new expansion allocation.

summary: FAIL — 1 critical, 4 major, 2 moderate findings. The primary critical is build drift: the decider's 2026-05-16T21:20 run (7 patches, including WIN-052 defense paragraph and SEC-4.6 five/six sweep) pushed successfully to git via workspace-sync rescue, but the HTML was never rebuilt. The live site at funwithscience.net/dome-model-review is stale by ~8h. Fix: run 'node build.js publish' from a clean clone. The four major tracker findings are: (1) next_id=395 collision-imminent with EXP-407 as live max — update next_id to 411 before any new allocation; (2) 4 EXP IDs in both live and archive — remove from live; (3) EXP-395 through EXP-398 are orphaned expansion files in git with no tracker entries; (4) EXP-409/410 are workspace-only and at loss risk. The two moderates (11 stuck-integrated live items, 9 stale-pending baby-orphans) are data-consistency drift that the next integration pass should clean up. All other checks pass: internal anchors, tab structure, external links, discoverability, progressive disclosure, dome-site WIN alignment (69 WINs on dome = our 69 base WINs), drift-audit fresh (18 candidates), no parse errors in curmudgeon reviews, no direction violations in workspace-sync.
